political.s: Delek US spent $180,000 on federal lobbying in 2023. Limited evidence of major PAC contributions compared to some energy sector peers. Conducts standard industry regulatory engagement.

charity.s: Delek demonstrates community engagement through initiatives like Salvation Army youth center support and Governor's Early Literacy Foundation partnerships via the Delek Fund for Hope. Emphasis on local community impact in operating regions.

environment.s: Delek settled a 2013 oil spill case for $2.25 million, paying penalties for failing to properly maintain Magnolia facility. EPA noted pipeline corrosion and poor facility maintenance. Records show Superfund site designation for refinery explosion. Environmental liability of $36 million recorded. History includes serious safety and environmental violations.

labor.s: OSHA investigation of 2008 Tyler refinery explosion killed 2 workers, injured 3 others, revealing systemic process safety management failures and substandard pipes. 2008 inspections cited multiple safety violations. Workplace injury history indicates below-average safety culture.

dei.s: No major public DEI initiatives or commitments identified in available sources. Company mentions diversity in hiring but provides limited specific DEI policy information or transparency.

animals.s: Not applicable for oil refining and logistics company.

guns.s: Not applicable for oil refining and logistics company.

privacy.s: No specific data privacy policies or practices identified in available corporate accountability sources. Information security role mentioned in company but limited transparency.

execPay.s: CEO Avigal Soreq received $7.68 million in total compensation in 2025 (88.3% bonus-based). Average employee salary approximately $75,000-$120,000 depending on source. CEO-to-worker pay ratio approximately 64:1 to 102:1.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
